name: San Salvador and Muro de Roda Loop
name_original: San Salvador y Muro de Roda – circular desde Aluján (ZZ-046)
description: This difficult 14.3-mile mountain biking route from Aluján offers challenging climbs, phenomenal descents, and stunning views of Muro de Rod
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around La Fueva are set within the Aragonese Pyrenees, offering diverse landscapes and extensive trail networks. The region is characterized by mountainous backdrops, fields, and meadows, with trails often traversing dusty red sand and striking rock formations. Riders can expect routes with good flow, technical descents, and challenging climbs. La Fueva is integrated into Zona Zero, a renowned mountain biking area known for its varied terrain. Best mountain bike trails around La Fueva The most popular mountain bike trail is San Salvador and Muro de Roda Loop, a 14.3 miles (23.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 32 minutes to complete. This difficult route features technical descents and views of historical structures. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Muro de Roda – Tierrantona loop from La Fueva/A Fueba, a difficult 24.0 miles (38.6 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs through a mountainous backdrop with sections of dusty red sand. Local mountain bikers also love the Tierrantona – Muro de Roda loop from La Fueva/A Fueba, a 12.6 miles (20.3 km) trail leading through fields and meadows, often completed in about 2 hours 9 minutes. Mountain biking around La Fueva is defined by dusty red sand, striking rock formations, and a mountainous backdrop. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in La Fueva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 6000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore La Fueva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in La Fueva?
A: La Fueva, integrated into the renowned Zona Zero network, offers a vast array of mountain biking opportunities. Within this guide, you'll find 16 curated routes, ranging from moderate to difficult, designed to showcase the region's diverse terrain.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in La Fueva?
A: While many trails in La Fueva feature technical descents and challenging climbs, there are options for varying skill levels. This guide includes 1 easy route and 10 moderate routes that can be more approachable for those with less experience or families looking for a less strenuous ride. The region is also developing routes specifically for e-bikes, which can help riders tackle technical ascents more easily.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in La Fueva?
A: Mountain biking in La Fueva is characterized by diverse landscapes within the Aragonese Pyrenees. You can expect trails with good 'flow,' technical descents, and challenging climbs, often traversing dusty red sand and striking rock formations. The mountainous backdrop, fields, and meadows add to the scenic variety.

Q: What are some notable circular mountain bike routes in La Fueva?
A: Many routes in La Fueva are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975520625/san-salvador-and-muro-de-roda-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Salvador and Muro de Roda Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 14.3 miles (23.1 km) long, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40315759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierrantona – Muro de Roda loop from La Fueva/A Fueba&lt;/a&gt;, covering 12.6 miles (20.3 km) through fields and meadows.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in La Fueva?
A: La Fueva is generally a great destination for outdoor activities. While trails are well-maintained, some may become muddy during wet periods. For the most enjoyable experience, consider visiting during drier seasons to avoid challenging muddy sections and fully appreciate the dusty red sand and rock formations.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: La Fueva is rich in history. You can encounter Romanesque heritage, including churches and defensive structures, and 16th-century fortified houses. Specific highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4446036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Samitier Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a religious-military complex with panoramic views, and the historical structures visible on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975520625/san-salvador-and-muro-de-roda-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Salvador and Muro de Roda Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or viewpoints near the mountain bike routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is strategically located near natural parks like Ordesa and Monte Perdido. Within La Fueva, you can find scenic views of the Mediano Reservoir, and for wildlife enthusiasts, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5954840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bearded Vulture Observatory&lt;/a&gt; offers a unique opportunity to observe these magnificent birds.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in La Fueva?
A: The mountain bike routes in La Fueva are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. Riders frequently praise the varied terrain, the good 'flow' of the trails, and the challenging yet rewarding climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, La Fueva offers several difficult routes for experienced mountain bikers. For a significant challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24315009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muro de Roda – Tierrantona loop from La Fueva/A Fueba&lt;/a&gt;, a 24.0-mile (38.6 km) path featuring challenging climbs through a mountainous backdrop. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Gorges and Lake – View of Ligüerre de Cinca loop from Palo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 27.7 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike trails in La Fueva?
A: The duration of trails varies depending on their length and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40315759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierrantona – Muro de Roda loop from La Fueva/A Fueba&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 2 hours 9 minutes, while the more difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975520625/san-salvador-and-muro-de-roda-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Salvador and Muro de Roda Loop&lt;/a&gt; usually requires about 2 hours 32 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there options for mountain biking near water features or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region features the scenic Mediano Reservoir, which offers beautiful views and a loop trail suitable for cycling. You can also explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Gorges and Lake – View of Ligüerre de Cinca loop from Palo&lt;/a&gt;, which provides views of gorges and a lake.
tips: Few 11th-century fortresses offer such views of the La Fueva valley and the Mediano reservoir. This old fortified town also houses a cemetery where the remains of relatives of inhabitants of the area rest, so special respect must be observed when visiting this historic site that transports us back in time.

This town on a low altitude plain is one of the few towns of non-defensive origin in La Fueva. One of the theories of the origin of its name is that these were the lands of someone named "Antonius" or "Antoninus".
The hermitage of San Salvador is located at the top of Cerro de Charo, the hill that offers us this wonderful descent.

faqs.question: What level of mountain biking skill is required for the San Salvador y Muro de Roda route?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as difficult and requires advanced riding skills and very good fitness. Riders should be prepared for challenging climbs and descents, with some sections potentially requiring you to push your bike. On the Singletrack Technical Scale (STS), it corresponds to S3-S6, indicating a high level of technical skill is needed.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 23 kilometers long and typically takes around 2 hours and 30 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied surface, including a significant amount of singletrack (11.3 km), unpaved sections, and some paved or asphalt parts. You can expect lots of rocks and loose material throughout the route, making it technically demanding.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in patrimonial sites and beautiful scenery. Highlights include the charming village of Charo, the historic Muro de Roda, and the church of San Salvador, which offers excellent views from its hilltop perch. You'll also pass through medieval villages and enjoy the scenic Descent to Aluján from San Salvador.

faqs.question: Is there parking available at the trailhead in Aluján?
faqs.answer: Yes, Aluján serves as the starting point for this circular route, and you should be able to find parking within the village to access the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: To avoid extreme temperatures and ensure the best trail conditions, the best times to ride are typically spring and autumn. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring snow or challenging conditions at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the San Salvador y Muro de Roda trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in this region, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially given the technical nature of the trail, potential wildlife, and to respect other trail users and local regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to ride this trail in Zona Zero Pirineos?
faqs.answer: This trail is located within the Zona Zero Pirineos network. While there isn't typically an entrance fee for the trails themselves, it's always a good idea to check the official Zona Zero Pirineos website for any specific regulations, recommended routes, or potential permits required for certain activities or areas, especially during peak seasons.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known mountain bike paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails, including the Sendero Histórico: Huesca, Sobrarbe. Ruta BTT 12.2 Variante Buetas – Morillo de Monclús - Tierra ntona, Senderos del Sobrarbe, and Sobrarbe. Ruta BTT 11. Circuito por Muro de Roda, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain biking trip on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, a first-aid kit, appropriate protective gear (helmet, gloves, knee pads), and navigation tools. Weather can change quickly, so layers are also recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenging sections or insider tips for tackling this route?
faqs.answer: The route is known for its 'savage' climbs and 'phenomenal' descents. Pay close attention to the technical singletrack sections, especially those with rocks and loose material. Some riders find starting early helps avoid the midday heat and allows ample time to navigate the more demanding parts without rush. Be prepared for sections where you might need to dismount and push your bike.

waypoints.name: Descent to Aluján from San Salvador
